title: Supply Chain Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: Demand forecasting and inventory-optimization calculations are increasingly automated, but network design and disruption strategy require judgment models can't yet own.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI forecasting and supply-chain optimization tools are credible and improving quickly, approaching the maturity of established analytics platforms.
rationales.adoption: Common at large manufacturing, retail, and tech-forward supply chains; spreading to mid-market operations.
rationales.agenticExposure: Forecast generation and scorecarding are agent-ownable; multi-tier network redesign and crisis response stay with a human analyst.

tasksAtRisk: Demand forecasting from historical and real-time data
tasksGrowing: Sustainability and ESG supply chain planning
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1081 (Logisticians); range reflects analyst-level positions within the broader logistician occupation

defenseSkills.skill: AI Supply Chain Forecasting & Optimization Tools

insight: Forecasting and inventory math are now largely AI-generated starting points, but building a network that survives a real disruption still takes an analyst who understands the business, not just the model. That keeps this role Moderate rather than sliding toward High.
